title: SIMP: Real-Time Energy and Time-Efficient 3D Motion Planning for Bio-Inspired AUVs
title_zh: SIMP：仿生AUV的实时节能与高效3D运动规划
abstract: Underwater navigation is an area of increasing research interest due to its fundamental complexity and industrial applications. However, due to convenience and current theoretical understanding, the vast majority of underwater platforms utilize thrusters, while other forms of propulsion, such as undulatory locomotion, have been given limited exposure. This paper provides the first real-time motion planning framework that produces energy and time efficient paths with empirical local optimality for articulated swimming robots in 3D, called SIMP. SIMP utilizes learned associations between parameterized dynamically feasible undulatory gaits with their expected energy cost, velocity, and swept-out volume of the robot during execution, to formulate a simplified optimization problem that decides the path to be followed with the corresponding consecutive gaits, and navigates the robot safely in complex 3D environments. The proposed pipeline is tested in numerical experiments with realistic dynamics for a 10 link underwater snake robot (USR) with anguilliform gaits, in simulated cluttered environments of significant challenge, displaying real-time replanning performance of more than 1 Hz.
abstract_zh: 水下导航因其基础复杂性和工业应用而成为日益增长的研究热点。然而，由于便利性和当前理论理解的限制，绝大多数水下平台使用推进器，而其他推进形式（如波动运动）受到的关注有限。本文提出了首个实时运动规划框架SIMP，能够为关节式游泳机器人生成能量和时间高效的路径，并具有经验局部最优性，适用于3D环境。SIMP利用参数化动态可行波动步态与其预期能量消耗、速度和执行过程中机器人扫掠体积之间的学习关联，构建一个简化的优化问题，决定要遵循的路径及相应的连续步态，并在复杂的3D环境中安全导航机器人。该管道在具有真实动力学模型的10连杆水下蛇形机器人（USR）上进行了数值实验，采用鳗鲡式步态，在模拟的杂乱且具有挑战性的环境中展示了超过1 Hz的实时重规划性能。
tldr_zh: 首个仿生AUV实时3D运动规划框架，实现节能高效路径规划。
cover_zh: 水下机器人也能像蛇一样游｜实时规划能耗直降
